summary: This page introduces the decomposition of any signal into its even and odd components using the identity f(t) = 1/2[f(t)+f(-t)] + 1/2[f(t)-f(-t)]. It then applies this result to the example f(t)=e^{-at}u(t), deriving explicit expressions for its even part and odd part and illustrating them in Fig. 1.24.

figures.caption: Fig. 1.24 Finding an even and odd components of a signal. The figure contains three plots: (a) a signal f(t), which is an exponential decay for t>=0; (b) its even component fe(t); and (c) its odd component fo(t).
